title: Flametrench Conformance Fixture
description: A single conformance fixture file: one capability, one operation family, one or more test vectors.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema for editor tooling.

spec_version.description: The specific Flametrench spec version this fixture file targets.

capability.description: Which Flametrench v0.1 capability this fixture exercises.

operation.description: Canonical operation name — matches the SDK/API method being validated (e.g. `encode`, `decode`, `check`). For state-machine fixtures, name the primary operation under test (e.g. `self_leave`, `accept_invitation`).

conformance_level.description: RFC 2119 level. MUST failures prevent an implementation from claiming conformance; SHOULD failures produce warnings; MAY is informational.

spec_section.description: Path + anchor into the normative spec prose that authorizes this fixture (e.g. `docs/ids.md#decoding-rules`).

description.description: One-line summary of what this fixture file validates.

shared.description: v0.2 only: optional fixture-level inputs that every test in the file shares. Harnesses MUST merge these into each test's input map before invocation. Useful for fixtures where 90% of input is constant (e.g. WebAuthn assertion keypair + RP ID) and only a few fields vary across tests.

Test.description: A single test vector. Either single-step (input + expected) or state-machine (users + steps).

id.description: Stable identifier for this individual test. Unique within the fixture file. Used for grepping and reporting.

input.description: Single-step form: the input arguments to the operation. Shape depends on the operation.

expected.description: Single-step form: the expected outcome of the operation.

users.description: State-machine form: declares named user variables. The harness pre-allocates a fresh `usr_` ID for each name; steps reference them via `{name}`.

steps.description: State-machine form: ordered list of operations the harness invokes against a fresh store. Each step's result MAY be captured for later substitution.

rules.description: v0.2 only: optional authz rewrite rules to register with the store before running the test. Keys are object_type values; each maps to a relation→rule map. Per ADR 0007.

RuleNode.description: A single primitive in a rewrite-rule union. v0.2 supports three node types: this, computed_userset, tuple_to_userset.

relation.description: For computed_userset: the relation to recurse with on the same object.

tupleset_relation.description: For tuple_to_userset: the relation traversed on the current object.

computed_userset_relation.description: For tuple_to_userset: the relation to check on each subject reached via the tupleset hop.

Step.description: One step of a state-machine test. Invokes `op` on the store with `input`. If `expected.error` is present, the step MUST throw the named error and the test ends. Otherwise the step MUST succeed; values from the result MAY be captured by name for later substitution.

op.description: Operation name, matching the SDK method (e.g. `create_org`, `self_leave`). The harness translates this to the language-idiomatic method name.

input.description: Operation arguments. String values matching the pattern `{var}` are resolved against the test's variable map (declared users + previous captures) before invocation.

captures.description: Map of capture-name → dotted-path-into-result. After the step succeeds, the harness extracts each value and stores it under the capture name for later substitution. Paths use dot notation: `owner_membership.id`, `invitation.org_id`.

Expected.description: Exactly one of `result` or `error` MUST be present. `result` asserts a successful return value; `error` asserts that the operation throws/raises an error of the named type.

result.description: The expected return value. Shape depends on the operation.

error.description: The name of the error type the operation MUST throw. Must match one of the spec-defined error names (e.g. `InvalidIdError`, `SoleOwnerError`).

error_matches.description: Optional: a substring the error's human-readable message MUST contain. Use sparingly — over-specifying message text makes fixtures fragile across SDKs with different phrasings.
